verses.text: Wisdom makes a man's face shine and softens his sternness; keep the king's command, for his word has power and none may question him. Wisdom honours authority. The wise know how to live prudently under those who rule.

Wisdom makes a man's face shine and softens his sternness; keep the king's command, for his word has power and none may question him. Wisdom honours authority. The wise know how to live prudently under those who rule.

Wisdom makes a man's face shine and softens his sternness; keep the king's command, for his word has power and none may question him. Wisdom honours authority. The wise know how to live prudently under those who rule.

Wisdom makes a man's face shine and softens his sternness; keep the king's command, for his word has power and none may question him. Wisdom honours authority. The wise know how to live prudently under those who rule.

He that keeps the commandment knows no evil thing, for the wise discern time and judgment; yet no man has power over the spirit or 'the day of death.' We cannot control our end. Obedience is wise, but death comes to all beyond our power.

He that keeps the commandment knows no evil thing, for the wise discern time and judgment; yet no man has power over the spirit or 'the day of death.' We cannot control our end. Obedience is wise, but death comes to all beyond our power.

Solomon saw the wicked buried with honour and soon forgotten in the city where they had done evil; this also is vanity. Earthly pomp covers many a wrong. The grave's honours cannot hide a life from God's reckoning.

Solomon saw the wicked buried with honour and soon forgotten in the city where they had done evil; this also is vanity. Earthly pomp covers many a wrong. The grave's honours cannot hide a life from God's reckoning.

Because judgment is not swift, hearts grow bold in evil; yet Solomon is sure 'it shall be well with them that fear God,' but not with the wicked. Delay is not acquittal. God's patience must not be mistaken for His approval.

Because judgment is not swift, hearts grow bold in evil; yet Solomon is sure 'it shall be well with them that fear God,' but not with the wicked. Delay is not acquittal. God's patience must not be mistaken for His approval.

He grieved that the just sometimes fare as the wicked and the wicked as the just; so he commends enjoying food and gladness in one's labour. Earth's outcomes are uneven. Until God sets all right, the believer thankfully enjoys His daily gifts.

He grieved that the just sometimes fare as the wicked and the wicked as the just; so he commends enjoying food and gladness in one's labour. Earth's outcomes are uneven. Until God sets all right, the believer thankfully enjoys His daily gifts.

Though he sought to know all God's work, he found 'a man cannot find out the work that is done under the sun,' even the wisest. God's ways exceed us. The wise rest in faith where their understanding fails.
